issue.title: Move WordPress-specific adapter bodies out of compiler core

artifacts.role: generic WPHX PHP compiler core retaining PHP IR, feature recording, file planning, bootstrap, manifest, and printer behavior
WordPress-profile adapter registry for bounded wp-http-* method body plans
grouped WP_Http helper compiler manifest proving eight adapter cases still pass
grouped WP_Http helper compiler receipt after profile registry split
operations documentation for compiler core versus WordPress profile registry responsibilities
architecture decision updated with profile registry boundary
program progress note for profile adapter registry cleanup

claims: WordPress-specific wp-http-* method body construction moved behind WphxPhpWordPressAdapters.hx.
WphxPhpCompiler.hx still owns generic PHP IR, feature recording, file planning, bootstrap, manifest writing, and printing.
The grouped WP_Http helper gate, buildCookieHeader gate, processHeaders gate, and public-shell snapshots remain green after the registry split.
non_claims: This does not claim WPHX PHP is a complete arbitrary-Haxe PHP backend.
This does not claim all WordPress adapter profiles are extracted or that future non-WP profiles exist.
This does not claim whole-file WP_Http ownership or WP_Http::request ownership.
